title: medicago lupulina subsp. willdenowii
intro: Medicago lupulina subsp. willdenowii is a subspecies of yellow sweet clover, a perennial legume native to Europe and western Asia, known for its bright yellow flowers and potential medicinal properties. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues due to its high content of isoflavones, particularly genistein and daidzein, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Medicago lupulina subsp. willdenowii include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as having potential anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal effects.
